summary: PR 2351 matched the lbcardgame object by changing lbcardgame source/data initialization, correcting a static header offset comment, updating symbol metadata, and flipping melee/lb/lbcardgame.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The loaded bot report claims four new matches for main/melee/lb/lbcardgame: .data, lb_8001CC84, lb_8001C658, and lb_8001C8BC.

key_files.role: Primary source changes for matching lbcardgame: expanded lb_803BAB74 initializer, refined unk_arr field types, adjusted OS time conversion code, and changed assignment shape in lb_8001CC84.
Static header correction for struct lb_80433318_t offset comment on _1C from +30 to +1C.
Build/matching status changed for melee/lb/lbcardgame.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Symbol metadata updates around lbcardgame-related data, including anonymous @ labels, resized lb_803BAB74 data object, and updated .data/.sdata/.sdata2 labels.

what_changed.text: lb_803BAB74's static initializer was expanded from a placeholder zero initializer to an explicit structured initializer with pad values, NULL pointer, seven repeated { 0x1F2C, 1, NULL } entries, and a final { -1, 0, NULL } entry.
The first two fields of lb_803BAB74.unk_arr entries changed from UNK_T to u32.
lb_8001C658 changed from manual OSGetTime()/OS_TIMER_CLOCK division to OSTicksToSeconds followed by OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds) before OSTicksToCalendarTime.
lb_8001CC84 removed an unused var_r3 local and changed lb_80433318.x10 = 0 to chained assignment lb_80433318.x10 = temp_r3 = 0.
lb_80433318_t offset comment for _1C was corrected from /* +30 */ to /* +1C */.
symbols.txt renamed several data/string symbols to numeric @ labels and resized lb_803BAB74 from size 0x28 to 0x78 while removing a separate lb_803BAB9C symbol entry.
The automated report recorded matched code +1328 bytes, linked code +3260 bytes, matched data +360 bytes, and four new matches for main/melee/lb/lbcardgame.

smart_moves.text: Used an explicit structured initializer for lb_803BAB74 instead of leaving a placeholder zero initializer, aligning source-owned data with the matched object.
Adjusted symbol sizes and labels in symbols.txt alongside the data initializer, including making lb_803BAB74 a 0x78 object, which matches the source structure becoming fully initialized.
Switched time conversion to SDK helper calls, OSTicksToSeconds and OSSecondsToTicks, rather than direct OS_TIMER_CLOCK division.
Kept the matching status change scoped to the lbcardgame object in configure.py.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For data-heavy matching, replacing placeholder initializers with a source-level struct initializer can close .data when paired with correct symbol metadata.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report new match main/melee/lb/lbcardgame .data 92.20% to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching time/date conversion code, SDK conversion helpers may produce the intended source shape better than manual division by OS_TIMER_CLOCK.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report new match lb_8001C658 99.96% to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: A chained assignment can be a narrow codegen tactic for matching when one local also needs to receive the reset value.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report new match lb_8001CC84 98.36% to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Offset comments in static headers matter for reviewability and should be corrected when the struct layout is known.

naming_conventions.convention: This PR used compiler-style numeric @ labels for multiple local/static data strings and literals in symbols.txt instead of module-prefixed or strN labels.
naming_conventions.notes: Observed in this PR only; not promoted as a global naming rule without curator review.

naming_conventions.convention: The source retained address-style names such as lb_803BAB74 and lb_80433318 rather than inventing semantic names for unclear globals.
naming_conventions.notes: Consistent with conservative naming when semantics are not proven by the PR evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote unknown initializer fields from UNK_T to concrete u32 where the initializer values are integral and the object layout is being matched.
Use SDK helper-call expression shape for time conversion rather than arithmetic division when matching calendar-time setup.
Use chained assignment to force both a state field and a local temporary to zero in one expression.
Update symbols.txt data object boundaries when a source initializer proves that adjacent data should belong to one object.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Structured data initializer plus symbol-boundary metadata can close a data match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2351, lb_803BAB74 changed from a placeholder zero initializer to an explicit structured initializer while symbols.txt resized lb_803BAB74 to 0x78 and removed the adjacent lb_803BAB9C entry; the bot report then listed main/melee/lb/lbcardgame .data as moving from 92.20% to 100.00%. Treat this as a candidate lesson for data-heavy matching, not a blanket rule to merge data objects without split evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: SDK time conversion helpers can matter for matching time/date code
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2351 changed lb_8001C658 from manual OS_TIMER_CLOCK division to OSTicksToSeconds plus OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds), and the bot report listed lb_8001C658 as newly 100.00%. This is a candidate tactic for similar OS time conversion functions.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a global rule that all lb/lbArchive or lb string symbols should become numeric @ labels; the PR only shows these specific symbols changed.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not claim independent objdiff/build verification beyond the loaded decomp-dev bot report.
Do not generalize chained assignment as preferred style; in this slice it is only a narrow matching tactic for lb_8001CC84.
